summary: Joel announces that two Fog Creek products won Software Development magazine's 16th Annual Productivity Awards. Fog Creek Copilot won in the Utilities category, while FogBugz 4.0 won in the Defect Tracking category. He credits the interns who built Copilot in a single summer. He also notes that Software Development magazine is merging with Dr. Dobb's Journal, pointing to the decline of developer print media.
key_points: Fog Creek Copilot, built by interns in one summer, won a Productivity Award in the Utilities category
FogBugz 4.0 won a Productivity Award in the Defect Tracking category
Productivity Awards are runners-up to the more prestigious Jolt Awards
Software Development magazine merging with Dr. Dobb's Journal signals the decline of developer print magazines
money_quotes: All credit for this cool award goes to the interns who developed it in one summer: Tyler, Ben, Michael, and Yaron!
Eric Sink's blog post about the eventual death of developer magazines seems prescient.
key_insight: A team of interns can build award-winning software in a single summer given the right environment, while the print media ecosystem that celebrates such achievements is itself fading away.
